guests.loadError: We couldn't load the guest list. Please try again.
guests.confirmRemove:
Remove "{name}" ({ip}) from the guest list?

Takes effect immediately: their live connections are terminated and every further request is rejected.
guests.confirmRemoveSelf:
"{name}" ({ip}) is YOUR OWN IP.

Removing it terminates this connection immediately and locks you out until the administrator re-approves you at the host PC. Remove anyway?
guests.removeError: We couldn't remove that guest. Please try again.
guests.approveHint: New IPs can only be approved in the desktop app on the host PC — never from here.

git.actDirty: Uncommitted changes in the working tree — merge is disabled.
git.graphHint: Newest at the top, oldest at the bottom. HEAD = where you are.
git.reviewHint: What a pull request would show: this branch's commits and changes since it left {base}.
git.reviewNoCommits: No commits yet on this branch.
git.reviewNoFiles: No file changes vs the base.
git.reviewTruncated: Showing the first results — the full list is larger.
git.reviewPatchTruncated: Diff truncated — too large to show in full.
git.reviewBaseLoadError: Couldn't load base branches; showing default only.
git.cleanHint: Everything is committed. Nothing has changed since the last save.

understanding.title: Understanding
understanding.prefill: Ask Claude to write its understanding first
understanding.prefillPrompt: Before acting on my request, first restate your understanding of it — the goal, the concrete steps you'll take, and any assumptions — in the `proposal.md` of an OpenSpec change (run `openspec new change <name>` first if one isn't started). Keep it current if the request changes, and let me confirm it before you proceed.
feature.kickoffPrompt: I want you to start a new feature. Make sure you are on the main branch synced with origin/main, then create a new feature branch. Start an OpenSpec change for it — run `openspec new change <name>` (or use /opsx propose) and draft its proposal. This is the feature we will be working on:
prompts.builtin.close.label: Close a finished feature
prompts.builtin.close: it works. Tick the remaining items in the change's tasks.md, run `openspec validate <change> --strict`, then `openspec archive <change>` to fold its delta into the living baseline. Then merge to main and make sure main is synced with origin.
prompts.builtin.close.legacy: it works. update our entry in plan.md . remove it from Active feature plans to recently shipped. merge to master - and make sure that master is synced with origin.
prompts.builtin.evaluate.label: Evaluate the different options we have
prompts.builtin.evaluate: Do you have only one idea/approach to this problem? If there are many approaches you can think of I want you to create a comparison table in the change's design.md where you will compare these approaches across a couple of dimensions. For example, difficulty of development, risk of development and so on. could you do that?
prompts.builtin.evaluate.legacy: Do you have only one idea/approach to this problem? If there are many approaches you can think of I want you to create a table in our plan where you will compare these approaches across a couple of dimensions. For example, difficulty of development, risk of development and so on. could you do that?
prompts.builtin.docsimplify.label: Document better understanding (but not diagrams)
prompts.builtin.docsimplify: The document is bloated right now. I want you to use appropriate principles for making it simpler, but not losing any important information. Use the principle of progressive disclosure - if the document covers many responsibilities, then try and refactor responsibilities into their own subfiles, that are only referenced by this document. You are trying to optimize for understanding of the operator. When you write the document - the goal should always be the most clarity for the reader.
prompts.builtin.walloftext.label: Wall of text warning
prompts.builtin.walloftext: You sent me a wall of text. Can you just give it to me concisely - what do you need from me. are you asking me a question? are you asking me for permission? please just tell me what you want from me concisely
prompts.builtin.understandingapp.label: understanding app
prompts.builtin.understandingapp: please serve me the understanding in the understanding app
feature.kickoffPrompt.legacy: I want you to start a new feature. make sure you are on the main branch that is synced with origin/main. create a new feature branch. Make a new entry in the Active feature plans section in plans.md . This is the feature we will be working on:
understanding.prefillPrompt.legacy: Before acting on my requests, first write your understanding of what I'm asking to `understanding.md` at the repo root, as markdown: a short restatement of the goal, the concrete things you'll do, and any assumptions. Keep it current if the request changes, and let me confirm it before you proceed.
prompts.title: Custom prompts
prompts.empty: No custom prompts yet. Add one below.
prompts.textPlaceholder: Prompt text — inserted into the chat box
prompts.paramsNone: No fields — add {{name}} to make a fill-in field.

plans.title: Plans
plans.empty: No plans yet. Create one below.
plans.noSteps: No steps yet. Add the first below.
plans.stepDetailsPlaceholder: Details — the prompt sent for this step
plans.stepEmpty: Add a name, details, or an expected result.
plans.pasteHint: Paste a block — lines starting PROMPT:, DETAILS:, EXPECTED RESULT: become steps.
plans.pastePlaceholder:
PROMPT: Scaffold
DETAILS: Create the module skeleton…
EXPECTED RESULT: Files compile, tests pass

PROMPT: …
plans.pasteEmpty: Couldn't find any steps to split.
notes.canvasPlaceholder: Your scratch canvas — jot down working notes you'll shape into a plan later. Drag the corner to make it bigger. Autosaves.
localapp.formBody: Which port does {name} serve on? The Local tab serves it through the harness, so it's reachable wherever you can log in — but never on the public home page.
localapp.saveError: Couldn't save the port. Please try again.
localapp.howTitle: Need to show a web app here?
localapp.howIntro: Most apps need a small one-time tweak to appear in this tab (serve the UI and API on one loopback port, with relative URLs). An agent can do it for you:
localapp.howStep1: Open an agent in the Claude Web repository (this app's own project).
localapp.howStep2: Give it the full path to the web app you want to expose.
localapp.howStep3: Ask it to reconfigure the app so it can be exposed through the Local tab — then enter the port it gives you above.
localapp.howNote: That's exactly how the apps already shown here were set up.
expose.someFail: {ok}/{total} checks pass — see what to fix below.
expose.error: Couldn't run the exposure check. Please try again.
expose.freshOk: Embed is current (not a cached copy).
expose.freshStale: Your browser is showing a stale copy of the embed.
expose.freshStaleDetail: Loaded {live}, but the current build is {server}. This is a browser-cache issue, not a product problem.
expose.freshBlankDetail: The embed rendered blank while the server is serving the current build ({server}). This is a browser-cache issue, not a product problem.
localapp.noProject: Select a project first — the Local tab shows the current project's app.
localapp.basicEmpty: This project doesn't have a running app to show yet.
